raw_consumer_statement:
In 2025, I filed several disputes with Citibank regarding charges from a scammer merchant who misrepresented their services and did not provide what I paid for. These were not fraud ( unauthorized charges ) I was scammed by the merchant. 
XX/XX/year> {$730.00} ( Case ID : XXXX ) : Citibank closed this dispute without conducting any investigation and without allowing me to submit evidence. 
XX/XX/year> {$550.00} ( Case ID : XXXX ) : Citibank denied this dispute with only a vague charge is valid response, refusing to provide merchant documentation or explain their decision. 
XX/XX/year> {$560.00} ( Case ID : XXXX ) : Citibank again denied this dispute with no meaningful explanation and no evidence provided from the merchant.

I requested clarification from Citibank, but they only sent vague no explanation responses. In each case, Citibank failed to conduct a reasonable, good-faith investigation as required under the Fair Credit Billing Act, and instead automatically sided with a scammer merchant.

I am also concerned that this behavior reflects a systematic practice at Citibank that harms consumers who are victims of scams.
